content:
# elizaos/eliza Weekly Report (May 10 - 16, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
This week, the ElizaOS development team focused on stabilizing local inference performance and refining hardware-specific runtime integrations. Significant progress was made in resolving speculative decoding compatibility issues for SWA-based and M-RoPE model architectures, ensuring system stability through proactive compatibility checks. Additionally, the project successfully scoped and integrated OpenVINO support for ASR tasks, while ongoing investigations into Vulkan bandwidth efficiency on Intel Lunar Lake hardware remain a priority for optimizing 14B-class model performance.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### Inference Runtime & Speculative Decoding
*   **SWA-based Model Support:** Resolved a critical silent failure where speculative decoding was disabled for SWA-based models (e.g., Qwen3.6) due to KV cache constraints. The fix propagates speculative types through compatibility probes, enabling `--spec-type dflash` functionality.
*   **M-RoPE Mitigation:** Implemented a mitigation patch in the `elizaOS/llama.cpp` fork to address conflicts between M-RoPE position validation and the speculative decoding harness. The system now performs upfront compatibility checks to prevent silent performance collapse.
*   **CUDA Stability:** Improved build stability by removing duplicate type specializations and adding missing dequantizer paths for `GGML_TYPE_Q1_0_g32`.

### Hardware Integration & New Features
*   **OpenVINO Integration:** Successfully integrated OpenVINO as a `LocalRuntimeKernel`. The scope was refined to prioritize ASR-side wiring (Whisper) on NPU-capable hardware, providing a 1.8× speedup over CPU-based inference.
*   **New Capabilities:** Introduced a native macOS Swift shell ([#7756](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7756)) and a new plugin, `plugin-ezpath`, which functions as a pay-per-request DEX meta-router for the Base mainnet ([#7735](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7735)).

## 🐛 Issues & Triage

### Closed Issues
*   **Speculative Decoding:** Resolved [#7635](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7635), fixing the fallback issue for SWA-based bodies.
*   **OpenVINO RFC:** Closed [#7633](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7633) following the successful integration of the backend foundation and ASR-specific wiring.

### New & Active Issues
*   **M-RoPE Compatibility ([#7631](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7631)):** Remains active. While a mitigation patch prevents performance degradation, full support for M-RoPE speculative decoding requires upstream changes to relax strict monotone-position requirements.
*   **Vulkan Performance Regression ([#7638](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7638)):** An ongoing investigation into a bandwidth-efficiency cliff for `Qwen2.5-14B` on Lunar Lake iGPUs. Current testing suggests the issue lies in kernel-selection heuristics rather than simple configuration toggles, marking this as a significant technical blocker for Intel-based deployments.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
The community demonstrated a high level of technical consensus regarding hardware acceleration, specifically in the decision to limit OpenVINO integration to ASR tasks to ensure optimal performance. Discussions surrounding speculative decoding were highly collaborative, involving deep-dive diagnostics into `llama-batch.cpp` and the development of fork-level patches to maintain system reliability. The introduction of new plugins and shell support indicates an active expansion of the framework's utility beyond core inference tasks.
